title: GET /api/v1/streaming/*: Server-Sent Events with auth and keepalive
description:
Implement SSE endpoints: /api/v1/streaming/user, /streaming/public, /streaming/public/local, /streaming/hashtag?tag=, /streaming/hashtag/local?tag=, /streaming/direct. Authenticate via Bearer token from Authorization header OR ?access_token= query param. Return text/event-stream. Subscribe to appropriate broadcast channel. Forward events. Send heartbeat comment (:heartbeat) every 15 seconds. Handle client disconnect by dropping the subscription.

AGENT WORKFLOW: Spawn a research subagent to connect to mastodon.social streaming SSE with curl and document the heartbeat format and exact header requirements. Spawn a testing subagent to write an SSE client test.

TEST DISCIPLINE:
- Connect to /streaming/user → receive update event when followed account posts
- Connect to /streaming/public → receive public post events
- Connect to /streaming/hashtag?tag=rust → receive event when #rust post created
- Heartbeat: after 15s of no events, receive ':heartbeat' comment line
- Auth: missing token → 401 before stream opens
- Disconnect: server cleans up subscription (no goroutine leak — verify via metrics or test helper)

DEFENSIVE PROGRAMMING:
- access_token query param: this is a valid auth method for SSE (Authorization header can't be set in EventSource)
- Gracefully handle broadcast::RecvError::Lagged: send a gap event or reconnect hint to client
- Connection limit: max 100 concurrent SSE connections (configurable) to prevent resource exhaustion
acceptance_criteria: SSE client receives correct events; heartbeat after 15s; missing auth returns 401; disconnect cleans up subscription; lagged receiver handled gracefully
